calc_crc16.name: calc_crc16
calc_crc16.canonical_plate:
Computes a CRC-16 checksum over a byte buffer using the CCITT /
XMODEM variant of the algorithm.
Polynomial: 0x1021
Initial CRC value: 0xFFFF
No input or output reflection, no final XOR.
Processes input one byte at a time, shifting 8 bits of state per byte.

compute_gcd.name: compute_gcd
compute_gcd.canonical_plate:
Computes the greatest common divisor of two non-negative integers
using Euclid's algorithm. Recursive implementation: gcd(a, b) =
gcd(b, a mod b), with gcd(a, 0) = a as the base case. No overflow
protection; inputs are assumed to be within a reasonable range.

get_stat_list_flags.name: get_stat_list_flags
get_stat_list_flags.doc_comment:
Read the flags bitfield from a D2 StatList structure.

Returns the 32-bit flags value at offset 0x10 after validating the
pointer is non-null and the magic signature at offset 0x00 matches
the StatList tag 0x01020304. Returns 0 on null-pointer or magic-
mismatch (corruption guard).

@param pStatList  pointer to a StatList structure
@return the flags at offset 0x10, or 0 if invalid
get_stat_list_flags.canonical_plate:
Read the flags bitfield from a D2 StatList structure. Validates
the list pointer is non-null and the magic signature at offset 0
matches 0x01020304 (the StatList tag). On success returns the
32-bit flags value at offset 0x10. Returns 0 on null-pointer or
magic-mismatch, guarding against reads from corrupted / non-
StatList memory.

get_stat_list_layer.name: get_stat_list_layer
get_stat_list_layer.doc_comment:
Read the layer field from a D2 StatList structure.

Returns the 32-bit DWORD at offset +0x04 (the StatList's layer
index). Performs NO validation — assumes the caller has already
verified pStatList is non-null and points at a well-formed
StatList. Callers that want the safety of magic-number validation
should use a validated variant instead.

@param pStatList  pointer to a StatList structure
@return the DWORD at offset +0x04 (layer index)
get_stat_list_layer.canonical_plate:
Reads the layer index (DWORD at offset +0x04) from a D2 StatList
structure. Unvalidated — directly dereferences the pointer with
no null-check and no magic-number validation. Callers must
ensure pStatList points at a well-formed StatList before
invocation. Returns the raw 32-bit value at +0x04.

get_stat_list_owner_guid.name: get_stat_list_owner_guid
get_stat_list_owner_guid.doc_comment:
Read the owner GUID from a D2 StatList structure.

Returns the 32-bit GUID at offset +0x20 after validating the
pointer is non-null and the magic signature at offset 0 matches
the StatList tag 0x01020304. Returns 0 on null-pointer or
magic-mismatch (corruption guard).

@param pStatList  pointer to a StatList structure
@return the GUID at offset +0x20, or 0 if invalid
get_stat_list_owner_guid.canonical_plate:
Read the owner GUID from a D2 StatList structure. Validates the
list pointer is non-null and the magic signature at offset 0
matches 0x01020304. On success returns the 32-bit GUID at
offset +0x20. Returns 0 on null-pointer or magic-mismatch,
guarding against reads from corrupted or non-StatList memory.

get_stat_list_prev_link.name: get_stat_list_prev_link
get_stat_list_prev_link.doc_comment:
Read the prev_link pointer from a D2 StatList structure.

Returns the 32-bit pointer at offset +0x34 after validating
pStatList is non-null and its magic signature at offset 0
matches 0x01020304. Part of a linked-list traversal: following
this pointer repeatedly walks backwards through the stat list
chain. Returns 0 on null or corruption.

@param pStatList  pointer to a StatList structure
@return the prev-link pointer at +0x34, or 0 if invalid
get_stat_list_prev_link.canonical_plate:
Read the prev-link pointer from a D2 StatList structure.
Validates the list pointer is non-null and the magic signature
at offset 0 matches 0x01020304. On success returns the 32-bit
pointer at offset +0x34 — this is the reverse traversal link in
a doubly-linked StatList chain. Following it repeatedly walks
backwards through the chain. Returns 0 on null-pointer or
magic-mismatch.

stat_list_add.name: stat_list_add
stat_list_add.canonical_plate:
Appends one stat entry to a StatList. Validates the list pointer
is non-null and that the list's magic number matches the expected
value (a corruption guard). If the list has capacity remaining,
writes the new entry at the next free slot, increments entry_count,
and returns 1. Returns 0 on overflow or validation failure.

advance_parser_state.name: advance_parser_state
advance_parser_state.canonical_plate:
Advances a small tokenizer-style state machine one input byte.
Given (current_state, input_byte) returns the next state. Letters
transition into an IDENT state, digits into NUMBER, single-byte
punctuation (',', ';', '.') emits PUNCT, and anything else resets
to INIT. IDENT and NUMBER are "sticky" while their character class
continues; mismatches fall back to INIT.

compute_str_len.name: compute_str_len
compute_str_len.doc_comment:
Compute the length of a null-terminated byte string.

Walks the input pointer one byte at a time until a zero byte is
encountered; returns the distance from start to the first zero.
Matches the semantics of standard strlen() but is a fresh
reimplementation (does not call into CRT). Behavior on a non-null-
terminated buffer is undefined.

@param str  pointer to a null-terminated C string
@return number of bytes before the terminating zero
compute_str_len.canonical_plate:
Computes the length of a null-terminated byte string. Walks the
input pointer one byte at a time until a zero byte is found, then
returns the distance from the start. Equivalent to the standard C
library's strlen() but implemented in-place (does not call into
the CRT). Behavior on a non-null-terminated buffer is undefined.
